hero.badge: One Altar · One Church · One Mission
hero.title: Rooted in Christ.
hero.description: Welcome to Cherubim and Seraphim Movement Church, Center for Deliverance through Evangelism. We are The Peculiar Cherubs. We are a growing family committed to worship, prayer, discipleship, evangelism, and transformed lives through Jesus Christ.

slides.alt: Special Apostle Pastor Alex Ogundipe ministering

identity.title: A church family for every people and generation.
identity.description: We exist to help people know Christ, grow in truth, discover purpose, and live as His disciples in everyday life.

hero.title:
Forming disciples.
Transforming lives.
hero.description: Peculiar Cherubs is a Christ-centred church family devoted to raising believers who know God, live His Word, and reflect His love.

story.title: A people called to be peculiar.
paragraphs: Cherubim and Seraphim Movement Church, Center for Deliverance through Evangelism, Kubwa, came about through a divine call and anointing on July 10, 2005.

The founding vision is that an end-time army should move in the power of God’s Word and deliver people from the bondage of the devil. Across every ministry and PDCM chapel, the church remains committed to worship, sound teaching, prayer, discipleship, evangelism, and compassionate service.
story.quote: You are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a peculiar people.

values.title: Christ Above All
values.text: Jesus is the centre of our message, worship, mission, and identity.

values.text: We are grounded in Scripture and dependent on the Holy Spirit.

values.text: Every person is seen, valued, loved, and invited into community.

issue-01.title: Walking in Purpose
issue-01.subtitle: Discovering God's Plan for Your Life
content: What does it mean to walk in purpose? It means matching our daily actions with God's design. In a world full of distractions, finding your calling can seem daunting. However, scripture assures us that God has pre-ordained good works for us to walk in.

Walking in purpose requires active listening and surrender. When we allow the Holy Spirit to direct our steps, even mundane tasks become opportunities for spiritual impact. This week, reflect on how your current roles can serve God's kingdom.

scriptures.text: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
For we are God’s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.

announcements: Join us for our Wednesday Shiloh Service at 6:00 PM as we continue our series on Purpose.
The Children's Ministry is recruiting teachers. Contact the parish office to register interest.

issue-02.title: Growing Through Prayer
issue-02.subtitle: Strengthening Your Connection to the Father
content: Prayer is not just a ritual; it is the lifeblood of the believer. It is the space where our weakness meets God's strength. Growing in prayer means moving beyond a checklist of needs to an intimate conversation of love.

Consistency is key to a robust prayer life. Just as physical exercise strengthens the body, setting daily moments aside to talk with and listen to God builds spiritual resilience. Let us cultivate a posture of constant communion.

scriptures.text: Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.

announcements: Monthly Hour of Praise and Deliverance will hold this Friday at 10:00 PM. Come ready for breakthroughs.
Publications team is accepting testimonies for the next monthly edition.

issue-03.title: Prepared for Impact
issue-03.subtitle: Equipping Believers to Change Their World
content: God does not call the qualified; He qualifies the called. Being prepared for impact means being willing to step out of comfort zones and be used as instruments of His grace in our schools, workplaces, and families.

Preparation happens in the quiet, daily moments of study, prayer, and service. When the opportunity arises, a prepared heart is ready to give an answer for the hope that lies within. Step forward with bold faith.

scriptures.text: Those who cleanse themselves from the latter will be instruments for special purposes, made holy, useful to the Master and prepared to do any good work.
But in your hearts revere Christ as Lord. Always be prepared to give an answer to everyone who asks you to give the reason for the hope that you have.

announcements: Registration for Pesach International Academy's new term is now open. See student services for details.
Youth Fellowship will hold a special outreach seminar this Saturday at 12:00 PM.

issue-04.title: Faith and Service
issue-04.subtitle: Faith Without Works is Dead
content: True faith is active, not passive. It expresses itself through service and love to those around us. When we serve, we reflect the heart of Jesus, who came not to be served, but to serve.
Every act of kindness, whether big or small, represents a seed of God's love sown into the community. Let us seek daily opportunities to be the hands and feet of Christ to those who are hurting.

scriptures.text: As the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without deeds is dead.
You, my brothers and sisters, were called to be free. But do not use your freedom to indulge the flesh; rather, serve one another humbly in love.

announcements: Feeding Ministry outreach will take place this Saturday morning. Volunteers should gather at 8:00 AM.
Weekly contributions for church development project can be submitted via the give portal.

issue-05.title: The Power of Praise
issue-05.subtitle: Worship That Breaks Chains
content: Praise is a powerful spiritual weapon. When we praise God in the midst of trials, we shift our focus from the size of our problems to the greatness of our God. Praise invites His manifest presence into our situations.

We see throughout scripture that praise often precedes victory. Let us make a habit of offering a sacrifice of praise, declaring God's goodness even when circumstances are challenging. Joy is found in His presence.

scriptures.text: Let everything that has breath praise the Lord. Praise the Lord.
About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God... Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake that the foundations of the prison were shaken.

announcements: Praise Night rehearsals will hold on Thursday at 5:00 PM for all choir members.
New believers class starts next Sunday immediately after the first service.

issue-06.title: Walking in Love
issue-06.subtitle: The Greatest Commandment
content: Love is the defining mark of a disciple. Jesus told us that the world will know we are His by our love for one another. This love is patient, kind, and does not keep a record of wrongs.

To walk in love is to choose forgiveness and empathy over pride and resentment. It is a daily decision that heals families, builds community, and shines God's light into a broken world. Choose love today.

scriptures.text: A new command I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you must love one another. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.
Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud... Love never fails.

announcements: Couples Fellowship is hosting a dinner seminar on family relationships on August 15. Register today.
House Fellowship coordinators meeting will hold after the Wednesday service.

sundaySchool.title: This Week’s Lesson
sundaySchool.text: Study notes, lesson objectives, discussion questions, and scriptures for Sunday School.

lectionaryCalendar.title: Follow the church’s scripture readings and teaching plan.
lectionaryCalendar.description: Browse the authenticated 2026 headquarters lectionary by month, including scripture texts, sermon topics, objectives, and special observances.

readings.topic: MERCY AS A GIFT OF GOD’S GRACE
readings.objective: Define the concept of mercy. Teach on how believers can access this gift of God’s grace and share the same in this hurting world. Using biblical examples, illustrate on a lifestyle marked by Mercy and the drawbacks of not embracing a lifestyle marked by mercy

What is the importance of mercy in relationships, and illuminate how God's boundless mercy is the foundation of every relationship. Using Ephesians 4:32, teach on Mercy’s Transformative Impact on family relationships. Highlight biblical Steps for nurturing a Mercy-Centered relationship, its benefits, and the effect of not doing so

Define wealth, kingdom wealth, and the essence of mercy in being a good steward. Using Acts 9:39, teach on how to steward kingdom wealth for an eternal reward. Identify biblical principles for unlocking kingdom wealth, including faith, generosity, and wise stewardship, and the disadvantages of not unlocking it.
readings.verificationNote: The source workbook lists January 20. Please confirm whether January 18 was intended.

readings.topic: PREPARING THE BRIDE FOR THE KING
readings.objective: Biblically illustrate who is the bride, who is the King, and explain the need to prepare for the coming of the King. Using Luke 15:11 explain how mercy functions in the context of repentance and its role in helping believers spiritually ready for the return of Christ. Teach on how to awaken believers to the urgency of preparing for the King and the danger of living un-prepared.

Explain mercy as the foundation for cleansing and true spiritual transformation. Teach the church how to move from brokenness to wholeness and continually grow through God’s mercies. Highlight how believers can cultivate a clean lifestyle, its benefits, and the drawbacks of an unclean life.

readings.topic: FORGIVENESS: THE CORNERSTONE OF FAMILY UNITY
readings.objective: Explain the concept of forgiveness in building strong, godly families. Using James 5:16, teach on how to build the value that entrenches open communication in the family. Discuss the dangers of unresolved offenses and bitterness within the family and the benefits of living a life of advanced forgiveness as stated in Col 3:13

Explore biblical principles of mercy in financial dealings and stewardship. Teach on how to practice generosity within and outside the household of faith. Highlight how unforgiveness can hinder financial fruitfulness and the benefit of forgiveness in financial fruitfulness.

Describe the concept of living above sin as a promise for believers in Christ. Highlight the practical steps believers should take to overcome temptation and live a life pleasing to God in this generation. Enumerate the advantages of God pleasers and the disadvantages of man pleasers in this end time.

Define righteousness, make a distinction between self-righteousness and God's righteousness. Teach on how righteousness is a gift received through grace. Enumerate the benefits of living as the righteousness of God and the disadvantages of living otherwise.

Establish the biblical foundation of parenting as a sacred covenant with God. Equip parents with spiritual and practical tools for raising righteous, godly children. List the benefits of entrenching righteousness as a family culture and the demerits of doing otherwise.

Explain the meaning and components of financial covenant. Teach Spirit-led financial stewardship rooted in obedience, generosity, and faith that unlocks financial blessings. Highlight the benefits of living a life of Spirit-led financial stewardship and the drawbacks of not doing so.

Teach the church on the King and the imminence of His return. Call the Church to purity, love, and faithful witness in anticipation of the second coming. Demystified the misconception around the return of the King and the benefit for believers to stay prepared.

Describe Jesus Christ as the gift of righteousness and trace the doctrine of righteousness from Old Testament typologies to New Testament fulfillment in Christ. Using Matthew 5:20, teach on imputed and practical righteousness as a spiritual principle for daily living. Enumerate the benefits of living daily as the righteousness of Christ and the demerits of not doing so.

Explain the redemptive power of mercy and its significance. Help believers understand how to access the redemptive power of mercy provided through the death and resurrection of Christ. Enumerate the benefits of enjoying the redemptive power of mercy and the demerits of not enjoying it.

Explain the role of mercy in healing and strengthening family relationships. Equip families with biblical tools for resolving conflict and nurturing godly values. Highlights the advantages of entrenching mercy as a core family value and its demerits if not entrenched,

Using Psalms 18:25 explain mercy as a currency, particularly as a believer's interaction with humanity and financial stewardship. Teach on how the act of mercy and generosity brings divine provision. Teach on how to explore opportunities to do good to the household of faith, Gal 6:10, highlighting the benefits and the drawbacks.

Explain the role of mercy as a divine strategy for believers’ preparedness in this end-time. Teach on how to live as citizens of God's kingdom, embodying values such as mercy in anticipation of the return of Jesus Christ. Highlight the use of mercy as a tool for evangelism to bring unbelievers to Christ and the missed opportunities of not using mercy as a tool for evangelism.

Explain the term abundant grace and describe grace as a person. Teach on the mystery of grace and how to receive and live in abundant grace. Highlights the advantages of living in abundant grace and the disadvantages of doing otherwise.

Using Matriarchs (Jochebed and Ruth) teach on the legacies bound by grace. Teach on how we can align with the generational covenant of grace and steps needed to pass down the legacies to the next generation. Enumerate the benefits of a family bonded by grace and the dangers of neglecting grace

Define the term sufficiency and the role of grace in accessing divine sufficiency. Teach on how to access divine sufficiency, exploring obedience and giving as some of the gateways to divine sufficiency. Identify the gains of living in divine sufficiency. Using Deuteronomy 32:15, list the dangers of times of comfort

Define on who grace is, explain His nature of empowering His Children. Teach on the place of grace in the spiritual empowerment for God’s service (1 Cor. 15:9-10). Explain the conditions for receiving God's empowering grace and warn about the consequences of resisting it.
Explain the mystery of grace and how grace manifests in our lives. Teach on how to unlock the mystery of grace. Highlight the benefits of unlocking grace and the demerits of not unlocking grace

Explain who the redeemed of the Lord is and the pathway to redemption. Teach about principles to retain and enjoy the redemption power granted unto men. Highlight the eternal and temporal benefits available to the redeemed of the Lord.

Describe what it means to be sought out and how Christ's love seeks out the lost in the family, no matter what. Teach on how parents should showcase Christ's love by not giving up on any of the family members. Highlight the benefits of praying for all relationship/family members, especially the unredeemed

Explain the term priest, and how God has called all into priesthood, with emphasis on the Father as the head of the family altar. Teach on the roles of fathers as a priest over his household. Highlights the dangers of neglecting the roles of the family altar priesthood
Explain to the Church who an overcomer is, the benefits on earth and in heaven. Teach on how to overcome in a world of crises and temptations. Highlights the dangers of a life that is not redeemed

Explain the concept of restored identity (Gen1:26) and the working of the Holy Spirit in achieving this. Using Simon Peter as an illustrative example, teach on how the power of the Holy Spirit restores. Identify and speak on the dangers of resisting change

readings.topic: RESTORED IDENTITY:  BUILDING A GODLY GENERATION
readings.objective: Teach the principle of building a godly generation. With reference to the Holy Spirit, teach on how to partner with the Holy Spirit to create, develop, and run a Godly home. Highlight the need for intentional discipleship under the leadership of the Holy Spirit within the family unit and the drawbacks of not.

Using 2 Sam 9:9-10, teach on the meaning of restored years and how God restores to the fullest. Using the Widow of Zarephath (I Kings 17:12-14) as an illustrative example, teach the church the principles of activating God’s restoration. Highlights the features of restoration and the benefits thereof

Using Matthew 28:19-20, explain the defining feature of the mandate. Teach on how end-time believers can leverage on the power of the Holy Spirit to fulfill this mandate. Enumerate the benefits of walking in the divine mandate and the danger of doing otherwise.

Identify and teach on the priesthood of the believers. Explain the steps to becoming a priest and the reason for the believers priesthood. Teach on the benefits of the priesthood and the dangers of otherwise.

readings.topic: THE FAMILY OF KINGS AND PRIESTS
readings.objective: Identify and teach on the attributes of the family of kings and priests. Teach on how a family can obtain, retain and sustain their kingship and/or priesthood. Teach on the benefits of sustaining family kingship and dangers of not doing so.

Identify and teach on the heritage of royals as related to wealth. Teach on the responsibilities of kingdom royals in relation to divine wealth. Teach on the benefits of the right usage of divine wealth and the dangers of doing otherwise

Teach the Children on the identity and calling of a Royal Priesthood. Teach the role of the parents on how to raise the character and practices of a Royal priesthood. Highlight the benefits of being a Royal Priest and the dangers of not.
Identify God's end-time armies and teach on their roles. Teach on how to be enlisted in the end time army of God. Highlight the benefits of being enlisted as God's end time armies.

Teach on what royalty is, and how to embrace your value and worth as a royal being. Using Esther, teach on the steps to transit from a servant mentality to a son or daughter mentality. Highlight the benefits of having the mind of Christ Jesus and the danger of not having it.

Define the term royal priesthood and the qualities of a priest. Teach on the functions of a royal priesthood in God's kingdom expansion. Highlight the importance of being a royal priesthood and the dangers of not being enlisted.

Explain what Abundant life is and its importance. Teach on how to access and sustain an abundant life. Demonstrate the benefit of unlocking the transformative power of abundant life in daily living and the danger of not unlocking same

readings.topic: FAMILY: EPITOME OF GOD’S ABUNDANT LIFE
readings.objective: Enumerate God’s purpose for family. Teach how a family can access and sustain abundant life in Christ Jesus. Explain the merits of families seeking God's abundant life collectively (Joshua 24:15.) and the drawback of not.

Define what true abundance is and that true abundance is not just material wealth but a life of peace, joy and purpose rooted in God. Teach on why true abundance is important and the benefits of living buoyantly in abundance. Explain the effect of not living in true abundance

readings.topic: THE ETERNAL NATURE OF ABUNDANT LIFE
readings.objective: Reflect on the eternal perspective of abundant life that is found in salvation through Jesus Christ and its relevance in modern society. Highlight how the principles of God's Kingdom, such as justice, mercy, and love, are essential for addressing challenges in the 21st-century society (Micah 6:8). Enumerate the features of abundant life that extend beyond the present, offering eternal hope and security in Christ.

Explain what mercy is and identify the nature of God's great mercies Highlight how great mercies serve as a foundation for God's redemptive plan Discuss the benefits of enjoying God's great mercies and the consequences of rejecting them.

Explain to the Church what peace is and what it is not; and the nature of Jesus’ peace (Isaiah 9:6, John 14:27). Teach on how a believer can walk in the realm of peace in this contemporary world and be victorious. Enumerate the benefits of aligning with Prince of peace and the dangers of departing from Him.

Enlighten the church on how peace of a godly family begins by surrendering every aspect of the household to Christ’s leadership. Explain how peace is sustained when discipline is balanced with love and respect is mutual among all family members. Give reasons why conflict is inevitable, but peace becomes a lifestyle when forgiveness flows freely in the family.
Explain the relationship between inner peace, wealth and contentment. Enumerate how financial peace is not just about personal blessing. Teach on the benefits of living a life of contentment
Teach on eternal peace and what it means to be secured in it. Explain how to be secured in eternal peace. Highlight the benefits of being secured in eternal peace and the dangers of not.

Differentiate between rest and God’s rest, and identify the characteristics of God’s rest. Teach on how a believer can access God's rest. State the benefits of enjoying God’s rest and the negative effects of not enjoying it.
Using biblical references teach on family altar preservation. Explain how to maintain family altar and its importance. Highlight the consequences of not preserving the family altar
Teach on what debt is and debt management. Highlight the principles of managing debt and their application. Enumerate the dangers of not managing debt and the benefits of debt management
Briefly explain what covenant is and covenant of rest (Exo. 33:14). Teach on how to enjoy the covenant of Rest. Enumerate the benefits of enjoying covenant rest and dangers of not

Teach on the biblical concept of rest and the need for rest in daily life. Using references explain God’s covenant of rest on every side and its purpose in the life of believers. Teach on the conditions to enjoy God’s rest and how to retain His rest.

Explain Christ as the Great Advocate and His role in the work of salvation. Teach on how believers can replicate in advocating for people without strive using the Jesus principle of love. State the dangers of rejecting Christ’s advocacy (John 1:11) and the benefits of accepting it (John 1:12)

Define parenting and identify the responsibilities of parents on children (biological and spiritual). Highlight how the parents can advocate intentionally for these godly heritage (with emphasis on not being a bad mentor). Teach the merits and demerits of advocating and not advocating for these godly heritages

Explain the nature of God in relation to His unending provisions (Psalm 68:19). Teach on what qualifies one to be a recipient of God’s provisions. Teach on the benefits of enjoying divine provision and how to maintain it.
Explain to the Church who a king is and what specific features that define Jesus' Kingship. Teach on the importance of King Jesus arrival to the world

readings.topic: THE ROLE OF BELIEVERS IN ADVOCACY
readings.objective: Explain what believers’ advocacy is and its importance. How can a believer advocate for the world. Highlight the danger of refusing the call to be an advocate mouthpiece for Christ, and benefits of being obedient.

readings.objective: Expound on the theme of Year 2027

hero.title:
Generosity that
advances the mission.
hero.description: Your giving supports worship, evangelism, discipleship, publications, outreach, church programmes, and the continued growth of PDCM chapels.

why.title: Giving is worship.
why.description: We give in gratitude to God and in partnership with the work He is doing through Peculiar Cherubs. Every gift helps the ministry serve people, share the gospel, and strengthen communities.

hero.title:
Grounded in truth.
Equipped for ministry.
hero.description: Christian Heritage Bible School exists to help believers understand Scripture, grow in spiritual maturity, and become equipped for effective Christian service and leadership.

introduction.title: Biblical education for purposeful Christian living.
introduction.description: Whether you are preparing for ministry, strengthening your biblical foundation, or seeking deeper spiritual growth, Christian Heritage Bible School provides structured teaching, practical resources, and a supportive learning community.

pathways.title: Register
pathways.text: Begin your application, review admission requirements, and submit your registration details.

pathways.title: Take Classes
pathways.text: Access current lessons, course materials, assignments, and class schedules.

pathways.title: Catch Up
pathways.text: Watch missed classes, download notes, and continue from where you stopped.

courses.title: Foundations of Christian Faith
courses.text: An introduction to essential Christian doctrines, salvation, spiritual growth, and faithful discipleship.

courses.title: Biblical Interpretation
courses.text: Learn responsible principles for studying, understanding, and applying Scripture.

courses.title: Christian Leadership and Ministry
courses.text: Develop biblical leadership character, ministry skills, and a heart for serving people.

courses.title: Evangelism and Missions
courses.text: Explore the biblical mandate for evangelism and practical approaches to sharing the gospel.

courses.title: Prayer and Spiritual Formation
courses.text: Build a disciplined prayer life and cultivate habits that deepen your walk with God.

courses.title: Church History and Heritage
courses.text: Study major movements, people, and events that have shaped the Christian faith.

studentServices.title: Student Dashboard
studentServices.text: See enrolled courses, class progress, announcements, and upcoming deadlines.

studentServices.title: Assignments and Assessments
studentServices.text: Submit coursework, complete assessments, and review lecturer feedback.

studentServices.title: Class Schedule
studentServices.text: View lecture dates, live-class links, academic calendar, and important notices.

studentServices.title: Learning Resources
studentServices.text: Download lecture notes, reading lists, outlines, recordings, and study guides.

studentServices.title: Academic Support
studentServices.text: Contact school administrators or lecturers when you need help with your studies.

studentServices.title: Certificates and Records
studentServices.text: Request academic records and access completion or graduation information.

registration.title: Begin your Bible-school journey.
registration.description: Complete the registration process to join the next Christian Heritage Bible School session. Official dates, fees, entry requirements, and payment information can be added here.

portal.title: Continue your learning.
portal.description: Returning students can access classes, catch-up recordings, assignments, learning materials, announcements, and academic support.

hero.title:
One mission.
Many expressions.
hero.description: Explore the chapels, schools, age-grade ministries, fellowship centres, and outreach expressions through which Peculiar Cherubs disciples, mentors, intercedes, teaches, serves, and reaches people.

mission.title: To disciple, to mentor, and to intercede.
mission.description: Peculiar Disciples of Christ Ministry is the church’s mission arm, charged with reaching the reached, the unreached, and those alienated from the church with the message of Jesus Christ.
mission.vision: A world free from bondage through the teaching of the Word of God.

mandates.title: Discipleship
mandates.text: Helping people believe in and follow Jesus Christ, while sharing the transforming work of Christ with others.

mandates.title: Mentoring
mandates.text: Advising, counselling, guiding, and encouraging people until they grow into the fullness of Christ.

mandates.title: Intercession
mandates.text: Standing in the gap, presenting people’s burdens to God, and praying for relief, restoration, and freedom.

groups.title: Standalone worshipping communities.
groups.description: Each chapel carries the shared mission of Peculiar Cherubs while serving its immediate community.

groups.title: Learning for faith and life.
groups.description: Structured biblical formation and child-centred education for different stages of life.

groups.title: Faith formation for every generation.
groups.description: Age-appropriate spiritual, educational, and psychological development.

groups.title: Church life beyond the auditorium.
groups.description: Community-based fellowship, compassionate service, prayer, and practical support.

pdcm-mission.title: Peculiar Disciples of Christ Ministry
pdcm-mission.summary: The church’s mission arm, committed to discipleship, mentoring, intercession, evangelism, prayer, and deliverance through Jesus Christ.
overview: PDCM draws its mission from Christ’s proclamation in Luke 4:18–19 and the Great Commission in Matthew 28:18–20.
It is responsible for reaching people with the message of Jesus Christ and bringing them into the Kingdom of God the Father.
functions: Preach the good tidings of Jesus Christ to all people.
Teach the Bible as the divinely inspired Word of God.
Assemble an end-time army in the power of God.
Preach the Word and deliver people from the bondage of the devil through Jesus Christ.

pdcm-gwarinpa.title: PDCM Gwarinpa Chapel
pdcm-gwarinpa.summary: A standalone PDCM chapel serving Gwarinpa through worship, discipleship, prayer, evangelism, and community.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Plot 14, Ken Saro-Wiwa Street, off Alhassan Dantata Road, 3rd Avenue Cornershop, Gwarinpa Estate

overview: The chapel began as Peculiar English Chapel at A. A. Bodunrin Hall, Kubwa, before relocating to Gwarinpa.
Following the relocation, it became Peculiar Disciples of Christ Ministry, Gwarinpa Chapel.

pdcm-english.title: PDCM English Chapel
pdcm-english.summary: An English-speaking PDCM chapel meeting every Sunday in the Church Auditorium, Kubwa.
overview: The chapel held its Inaugural and Proclamation Service on Sunday, 6 February 2022.
It provides an English-language worship and discipleship expression within the Peculiar Cherubs family.

pdcm-byazhin.title: PDCM Byazhin Chapel
pdcm-byazhin.summary: A PDCM worshipping community serving Byazhin Across and surrounding areas.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Behind Football Field Area, Byazhin Across, by Kubwa

facts.label: Formation
facts.value: Following a week-long revival and commendation service

overview: PDCM Byazhin Chapel was inaugurated after a week-long revival and commendation service.
The chapel serves believers and families through worship, prayer, the Word, evangelism, and fellowship.

pdcm-mega-youth.title: PDCM Mega Youth Chapel
pdcm-mega-youth.summary: A standalone church for adults aged 20–40, raising disciples, leaders, worshippers, volunteers, and witnesses for Christ.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: A. A. Bodunrin Hall, Church Complex, Kubwa

overview: The Peculiar Youth Ministry was upgraded to a full-fledged chapel and renamed PDCM Mega Youth Chapel.
Its programmes support spiritual, educational, psychological, leadership, vocational, and relational development.

leaders.name: Special Mother in Israel Pastor Dayo Arogundade

functions: Spiritual, educational, and psychological development for adults aged 20–40.
Evangelism training and participation in mission projects.
Volunteer service within the church and wider community.
Leadership training through small groups, teaching, and practical service.
Career, relationship, marriage, and life guidance and counselling.
Participation in district, national, and wider Christian youth programmes, camps, conventions, and retreats.

bible-college.title: Christian Heritage Bible School
bible-college.summary: Structured biblical education with registration, classes, catch-up lessons, assignments, resources, and student support.

pesach-academy.title: PESACH International Academy
pesach-academy.summary: A child-centred school in Kubwa offering Crèche, Nursery, and Primary education.

facts.label: Admissions
facts.value: Admission information available through the school

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Plot 53A/54A, 2/2 Junction, Hamza Abdullahi Road, off Gado Nasko Road, Kubwa, Abuja

overview: PESACH International Academy provides child-centred education in a structured learning environment.
The academy combines classroom learning, extracurricular development, technology, and practical support for families.

children-ministry.title: Children Ministry
children-ministry.summary: Spiritual, educational, and psychological development for children from birth to age 12.
overview: The ministry provides inclusive, age-appropriate Christian formation for children.
Children who can read and write are encouraged to attend with their Bible and writing materials.
functions: Coordinate the national curriculum with teachers and ministry workers.
Organize training for teachers and handlers.
Connect classes to sermons and the 12-noon prayer.
Organize camps, excursions, summer schools, and other developmental activities.
Arrange children into appropriate classes for easier learning.

teenage-ministry.title: Teenage Ministry
teenage-ministry.summary: Spiritual, educational, and psychological development for teenagers aged 13–19.
overview: Teenagers are grouped into structured classes with class prefects and a curriculum designed around their needs.
The ministry teaches faith in Christ, C&S liturgy, hymnals, prayer, worship, and practical Christian living.
functions: Organize annual camps, excursions, summer school, and youth activities.
Provide interactive platforms where teenagers can discuss their challenges.
Prepare older teenagers for national youth programmes and church activities.
Encourage every teenager to attend with a Bible and writing materials.

house-fellowships.title: House Fellowship Centres
house-fellowships.summary: Twenty community-based fellowship centres serving neighbourhoods across Abuja and surrounding areas.

feeding-ministry.title: Jesus Loves You Feeding Ministry
feeding-ministry.summary: A compassionate outreach providing food and palliatives to less-privileged and indigent people in communities around the church and Abuja.

facts.label: Service area
facts.value: Communities around the churches and Abuja

overview: Jesus Loves You Feeding Ministry was established to provide practical support to people experiencing need.
The ministry is sustained through voluntary participation and generosity.
functions: Mobilize voluntary sponsors and ministry partners.

hero.title:
Worship. Gather.
Grow together.
hero.description: Explore the Peculiar Cherubs calendar, see what is coming next, and catch up on past programmes and celebrations.
